rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ADELIA E. INGRAM LIVNG TRUST OF 2013 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Reconsideration; Memo Of Points And Authorities; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] The court declines Objectors' request to order further sanctions under CCP section 128.7. Such sanctions are available by way of separate motion only; in any event, there's no evidence that Objectors gave Ms. Lee the required notice and opportunity to withdraw her motion. (See CCP section 128.7, subd. (c)(1).) At this time, the court declines to act on its own motion. (See CCP section 128.7, subd. (c)(2).) Ms. Lee is ordered to pay to Objectors $1,840, her portion of the total sanctions originally awarded to Objectors in the December 18, 2020 order of the court. Sanctions are to be paid within 10 days of notice of entry of this order. Objectors shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on March 24, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ADELIA E. INGRAM LIVNG TRUST OF 2013 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration; Memo Of Points And Authorities; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Attorney Elaine Lee's Motion for Reconsideration of the court's December 18, 2020 order in this matter is DENIED. Ms. Lee-now Petitioner's former counsel-was sanctioned $1,840 (2/3rds of the total sanctions award) under CCP sections 2023.010 and 2023.030 subd. (a) for her failure to respond as required to inspection demands and for subsequent failure to produce documents or otherwise respond with meritorious objections, without substantial justification, in August 2020. Though served with the underlying motion to compel further production and for sanctions, Ms. Lee did not file an opposition to it, and though she reviewed the court's tentative ruling, she did not appear to contest it. Instead, she filed this motion for reconsideration. As the moving party on a section 1008 motion for reconsideration, Ms. Lee has the burden of filing the required affidavit with the required elements, and showing both "new or different" facts, law, or circumstances warranting reconsideration of the order and a sufficient explanation for why the new or different information was not offered before the order issued. (see CCP section 1008, Pazderka v. Caballeros Dimas Alang, Inc. (1998) 62 Cal. App. 4th 658, 670.) Although she submits an affidavit and offers information she did not offer at the hearing on the motion to compel further responses and for sanctions, there is no showing that what is offered in this motion for reconsideration is "new" facts or circumstances, and Ms. Lee does not offer a satisfactory explanation for why she did not present this information - or any opposition at all - in response to the motion to compel further response and for sanctions to the December 17, 2020 tentative ruling that awarded Objectors sanctions against both Ms. Lee and Mr. Simon for misuse of the discovery process. Further, even if the court were to accept the information offered in the motion for reconsideration as "new or different" and the explanation for failure to present it earlier as sufficient (the court does not), the information that Ms. Lee knowingly withheld discovery from both new counsel and the propounding party until mid-November - two months after Mr. Simon had substituted in as new counsel - does nothing to change the court's original findings that she misused the discovery process under CCP section 2023.010 and 2023.030 subd. (a) in August 2020 so as to warrant sanctions. (see December 18, 2020 Order at 2:14-3:2.) [Part 1 of 2]
